meta.title: Striver's SDE Sheet – Top Coding Interview Problems
meta.author: Raj Vikramaditya (Striver)

questions.name: Set Matrix Zeroes
questions.description: Given an m×n matrix, if any element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0. Do it in-place. The key challenge is to not use extra O(m+n) space — instead use the first row and column as markers.

questions.name: Pascal's Triangle
questions.description: Given an integer numRows, generate the first numRows rows of Pascal's Triangle. Each number is the sum of the two numbers directly above it. Row i has i+1 elements, and each row starts and ends with 1.

questions.name: Next Permutation
questions.description: Given an array of integers, rearrange its elements to form the lexicographically next greater permutation. If no greater permutation exists, rearrange it as the lowest possible order (sorted ascending). Must be done in-place with O(1) extra space.

questions.name: Kadane's Algorithm / Maximum Subarray
questions.description: Given an integer array nums, find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) which has the largest sum and return its sum. Classic application of Kadane's Algorithm with O(n) time complexity.

questions.name: Sort Colors (Sort 0s, 1s, 2s)
questions.description: Given an array nums with n objects colored red (0), white (1), or blue (2), sort them in-place so that objects of the same color are adjacent. Uses the Dutch National Flag algorithm with three pointers in a single pass.

questions.name: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
questions.description: Given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on day i, find the maximum profit from a single buy-sell transaction. You must buy before you sell. If no profit is possible, return 0.

questions.name: Rotate Image
questions.description: Given an n×n 2D matrix representing an image, rotate the image by 90 degrees clockwise in-place. The trick is to first transpose the matrix (swap matrix[i][j] with matrix[j][i]), then reverse each row.

questions.name: Merge Intervals
questions.description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals. Sort by start time, then greedily merge.

questions.name: Merge Sorted Array
questions.description: You are given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, sorted in non-decreasing order, and two integers m and n. Merge nums2 into nums1 as one sorted array in-place. The trick is to fill from the end to avoid overwriting.

questions.name: Find the Duplicate Number
questions.description: Given an array of integers nums containing n+1 integers where each integer is in the range [1, n], find the one duplicate number. Must use O(1) extra space — solved optimally using Floyd's cycle detection (tortoise and hare).

questions.name: Count Inversions
questions.description: Count the number of inversions in an array. An inversion is a pair (i, j) such that i < j but arr[i] > arr[j]. The efficient O(n log n) approach uses a modified merge sort to count inversions during the merge step.

questions.name: Search a 2D Matrix
questions.description: Given an m×n integer matrix where each row is sorted left-to-right and the first integer of each row is greater than the last integer of the previous row, return true if target exists. Treat the matrix as a flattened sorted array and apply binary search.

questions.name: Majority Element (> N/2 times)
questions.description: Given an array nums of size n, return the majority element — the element that appears more than ⌊n/2⌋ times. The optimal solution uses Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm which runs in O(n) time and O(1) space.

questions.name: Majority Element (> N/3 times)
questions.description: Given an integer array of size n, find all elements that appear more than ⌊n/3⌋ times. There can be at most two such elements. Uses the extended Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm with two candidate counters.

questions.name: Grid Unique Paths
questions.description: A robot is located at the top-left corner of an m×n grid. It can only move either down or right. Count the number of unique paths to reach the bottom-right corner. Solved with DP or combinatorics: C(m+n-2, m-1).

questions.name: Reverse Pairs
questions.description: Given an integer array nums, return the number of reverse pairs, i.e., pairs (i, j) where i < j and nums[i] > 2 * nums[j]. The efficient O(n log n) solution modifies merge sort to count valid pairs during the merge step.

questions.name: Two Sum
questions.description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers that add up to target. Each input has exactly one solution and you may not use the same element twice. Solved in O(n) using a hash map.

questions.name: 4-Sum
questions.description: Given an array of n integers, find all unique quadruplets [nums[a], nums[b], nums[c], nums[d]] such that they sum to the given target. Extension of 3Sum — sort the array, fix two pointers, and use two-pointer technique for the inner pair.

questions.name: Longest Consecutive Sequence
questions.description: Given an unsorted array of integers, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence. Must run in O(n) time. Use a hash set: for each number that is the start of a sequence (num-1 not in set), count the chain length.

questions.name: Largest Subarray with 0 Sum
questions.description: Given an array of integers, find the length of the longest subarray with sum equal to 0. Uses prefix sum with a hash map — store the first occurrence of each prefix sum, and when seen again, the subarray between them has sum 0.

questions.name: Count Substrings with Equal 0s and 1s
questions.description: Given a binary string S, count the number of substrings that have equal numbers of 0s and 1s. Replace 0s with -1s and find subarrays with sum 0 using prefix sum — equivalent to counting pairs of equal prefix sums.

questions.name: XOR Queries of a Subarray
questions.description: Given an integer array arr and an array of queries where queries[i] = [L, R], return the XOR of elements between indices L and R (inclusive) for each query. Build a prefix XOR array so each query is answered in O(1).

questions.name: Longest Subarray with Sum K (Positives + Negatives)
questions.description: Given an array that may contain both positive and negative integers and a target sum K, find the length of the longest subarray with sum exactly K. Uses prefix sum with a hash map. Works even with negatives unlike the sliding window approach.

questions.name: 3-Sum
questions.description: Given an integer array nums, return all triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i ≠ j ≠ k and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0, with no duplicate triplets. Sort the array, fix one element, and use two pointers for the remaining pair.

questions.name: Reverse Linked List
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 5000]
questions.description: Reverse a singly linked list. Can be done iteratively with three pointers (prev, curr, next) in O(n) time and O(1) space, or recursively. A fundamental linked list operation used as a building block in many harder problems.

questions.name: Middle of Linked List
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [1, 100]
questions.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the middle node. If there are two middle nodes, return the second. Uses the slow-fast pointer (tortoise and hare) technique — slow moves one step, fast moves two steps.

questions.name: Merge Two Sorted Lists
constraints: The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50]
questions.description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return the head of the merged sorted list. Uses a dummy head node and a pointer that advances along whichever list has the smaller current value. O(n+m) time, O(1) extra space.

questions.name: Remove Nth Node From End of List
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is sz
questions.description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head. Uses two pointers with a gap of n — when the fast pointer reaches the end, the slow pointer is just before the target node.

questions.name: Delete Node in a Linked List
constraints: The number of the nodes in the given list is in the range [2, 1000]
questions.description: Given access only to a node (not the head) in a linked list, delete that node. Since you can't access the previous node, copy the value of the next node into the current node and delete the next node.

questions.name: Add Two Numbers
constraints: The number of nodes in each linked list is in the range [1, 100]
It is guaranteed that the list represents a number that does not have leading zeros
questions.description: Given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers stored in reverse order (each node contains a single digit), add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list in the same format. Handle carry carefully.

questions.name: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
questions.description: Given the heads of two singly linked lists, return the node at which the two lists intersect, or null if they don't. The two-pointer trick: after reaching the end, each pointer switches to the other list's head — they meet at the intersection.

questions.name: Linked List Cycle
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 10^4]
pos is -1 or a valid index
questions.description: Given the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it. Uses Floyd's cycle detection: slow pointer moves one step, fast pointer moves two steps. If they ever meet, a cycle exists.

questions.name: Reverse Linked List II
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is n
questions.description: Given the head of a singly linked list and two integers left and right, reverse the nodes from position left to right. Uses careful pointer manipulation — connect the reversed segment back to both ends of the unchanged portions.

questions.name: Check Palindrome Linked List
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [1, 10^5]
questions.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return true if it is a palindrome, or false otherwise. Optimal approach: find middle, reverse second half, compare both halves, then optionally restore the list.

questions.name: Flatten a Multilevel Doubly Linked List
constraints: The number of Nodes will not exceed 1000
questions.description: Given the head of a special linked list where some nodes have an additional child pointer to another linked list, flatten it into a single-level doubly linked list. Uses recursion or a stack to handle nested child lists.

questions.name: Rotate List
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 500]
questions.description: Given the head of a linked list, rotate the list to the right by k places. Connect the list into a ring, find the new tail (at position len - k % len - 1), and break the ring to form the rotated list.

questions.name: 3-Sum (Linked List variant / Close Sum)
questions.description: Given an integer array nums and an integer target, find three integers whose sum is closest to target. Sort the array and use a two-pointer approach. Track the minimum absolute difference from target across all triplets.

questions.name: Trapping Rain Water
questions.description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining. Optimal O(n) solution uses two pointers tracking max-left and max-right heights.

questions.name: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
constraints: nums is sorted in non-decreasing order
questions.description: Given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order, remove duplicates in-place such that each unique element appears only once. Return the number k of unique elements. Uses a slow pointer that only advances on new values.

questions.name: Maximum Consecutive Ones
questions.description: Given a binary array nums, return the maximum number of consecutive 1s in the array. Single pass — maintain a running count that resets to 0 on encountering a 0, tracking the global maximum.

questions.name: Copy List with Random Pointer
constraints: Node.random is null or pointing to some node in the linked list
questions.description: Construct a deep copy of a linked list where each node contains an additional random pointer which could point to any node in the list or null. The O(n) space approach uses a hash map; the O(1) space approach interleaves copied nodes.

questions.name: Find the Starting Point of Loop in Linked List
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 10^4]
questions.description: Given the head of a linked list that may contain a cycle, return the node where the cycle begins, or null if there is no cycle. Uses Floyd's algorithm: after detecting the cycle, reset one pointer to head and move both one step at a time — they meet at the entry point.

questions.name: N Meetings in One Room
questions.description: Given n meetings with start and end times, find the maximum number of meetings that can be held in a single room (only one meeting at a time). Greedy: sort by end time, always pick the meeting that ends earliest and doesn't overlap with the last selected.

questions.name: Minimum Number of Platforms
questions.description: Given arrival and departure times of trains at a station, find the minimum number of platforms required so no train waits. Sort both arrays; use a two-pointer sweep to track the maximum simultaneous trains at any point.

questions.name: Job Sequencing Problem
questions.description: Given a list of jobs each with a deadline and profit, find the maximum number of jobs and total profit achievable if each job takes exactly one unit of time. Greedy: sort by profit descending, assign each job to the latest available slot before its deadline.

questions.name: Fractional Knapsack
questions.description: Given items with weights and values and a knapsack capacity W, maximize the value you can carry. Unlike 0/1 knapsack, you can take fractions. Greedy: sort by value-to-weight ratio descending and fill greedily.

questions.name: Greedy Algorithm to find Minimum Number of Coins
questions.description: Given a value V, find the minimum number of coins required to make change using Indian currency denominations {1,2,5,10,20,50,100,200,500,2000}. Greedy works here because these denominations satisfy the greedy property.

questions.name: Activity Selection
questions.description: Given a collection of intervals, find the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest non-overlapping. Equivalent to finding the maximum number of non-overlapping intervals (activity selection) and subtracting from total.

questions.name: Subset Sums
questions.description: Given an array of integers, return all possible subset sums. Generate all 2^n subsets using recursion/bitmasking, compute the sum of each, and return them in sorted order.

questions.name: Subsets II (with duplicates)
questions.description: Given an integer array nums that may contain duplicates, return all possible subsets (the power set) with no duplicate subsets. Sort the input first, then in the recursive backtracking, skip duplicate elements at the same level.

questions.name: Combination Sum I
questions.description: Given an array of distinct integers and a target, find all unique combinations where the chosen numbers sum to target. You may use the same number unlimited times. Backtrack with a start index to avoid re-using earlier elements.

questions.name: Combination Sum II
questions.description: Given a collection of candidate numbers (may contain duplicates) and a target, find all unique combinations that sum to target where each candidate can only be used once. Sort and skip duplicates at the same recursion level.

questions.name: Palindrome Partitioning
constraints: s contains only lowercase English letters
questions.description: Given a string s, partition it so that every substring of the partition is a palindrome. Return all possible palindrome partitioning. Backtrack — at each index try all valid palindrome prefixes and recurse on the remainder.

questions.name: K-th Permutation Sequence
questions.description: Given n and k, return the kth permutation sequence of numbers 1..n. Instead of generating all n! permutations, use the factorial number system: at each position, determine which digit belongs there using (k-1) / (n-1)!.

questions.name: Permutations (Duplicates not allowed)
questions.description: Given an array of distinct integers, return all possible permutations. Classic backtracking — at each position, try swapping each remaining element in, recurse, then swap back to restore state.

questions.name: Permutations (with duplicates)
questions.description: Given a collection of numbers that might contain duplicates, return all possible unique permutations. Sort the array, then during backtracking skip duplicates: if nums[i] == nums[i-1] and nums[i-1] is not used, skip.

questions.name: N-Queens
questions.description: Place n queens on an n×n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Classic backtracking — for each row, try placing a queen in each column, checking that no previous queen shares the same column or diagonal.

questions.name: Sudoku Solver
constraints: It is guaranteed the input board has only one solution
questions.description: Given a partially filled 9×9 Sudoku board, solve it by filling the empty cells. Each row, column, and 3×3 box must contain digits 1–9 without repetition. Pure backtracking — try digits 1–9 at each empty cell and backtrack on conflict.

questions.name: M Coloring Problem
questions.description: Given an undirected graph and m colors, determine if the graph can be colored with at most m colors such that no two adjacent vertices share the same color. Classic constraint satisfaction solved with backtracking.

questions.name: Rat in a Maze
questions.description: Given an n×n binary matrix, find all paths for a rat to reach from (0,0) to (n-1,n-1). The rat can move in four directions (U,D,L,R) and can only step on cells with value 1. Backtracking with a visited matrix to avoid revisiting cells.

questions.name: N-th Root of an Integer
questions.description: Given two positive integers n and m, find the nth root of m (i.e., x such that x^n = m). If no integer answer exists, return -1. Binary search on the answer in range [1, m].

questions.name: Matrix Median
constraints: R and C are always odd
questions.description: Given a row-wise sorted matrix with an odd number of rows and columns, find the overall median. Binary search on the value range — for a given mid value, count elements ≤ mid using binary search on each row.

questions.name: Find the Element that Appears Once in a Sorted Array
questions.description: Given a sorted array where every element appears exactly twice except for one element which appears once, find that single element. Binary search — the single element disrupts the expected even-index pattern.

questions.name: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
questions.description: Given a rotated sorted array with all unique elements, search for a target value. Binary search — at each mid, determine which half is sorted, then check if target lies in that half.

questions.name: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
questions.description: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n, find the median of the combined sorted array. Must run in O(log(min(m,n))). Binary search on the smaller array to find the correct partition of both arrays.

questions.name: K-th Element of Two Sorted Arrays
questions.description: Given two sorted arrays of size n and m, find the kth element in their merged sorted order. Binary search: compare elements at position min(k/2, size) in each array and eliminate the smaller half.

questions.name: Allocate Books (Book Allocation Problem)
questions.description: Given an array where each element represents the number of pages in a book, and m students, allocate books to minimize the maximum pages assigned to any student. Binary search on the answer — check feasibility with a greedy allocation.

questions.name: Aggressive Cows
questions.description: Given n stall positions and c cows, place the cows to maximize the minimum distance between any two cows. Binary search on the minimum distance — check if c cows can be placed with at least that distance apart.

questions.name: Max Heap / Min Heap
questions.description: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array without sorting the full array. Use a min-heap of size k — iterate through the array, maintaining only the k largest elements. The heap top is the answer.

questions.name: Top K Frequent Elements
questions.description: Given an integer array and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements. Use a bucket sort (frequency array of size n+1) or a min-heap of size k with frequency counts.

questions.name: Find Median from Data Stream
constraints: At most 5 * 10^4 calls to addNum and findMedian
questions.description: Design a data structure that supports adding numbers and finding the median at any point. Maintain two heaps: a max-heap for the lower half and a min-heap for the upper half, balanced to differ by at most one element.

questions.name: Merge K Sorted Lists
questions.description: Given an array of k linked lists, each sorted in ascending order, merge them into one sorted linked list. Optimal approach uses a min-heap of size k — repeatedly extract the minimum element and advance that list's pointer.

questions.name: Replace each array element by its corresponding rank
questions.description: Replace each element in an array by its rank (1 for smallest, 2 for second smallest, etc.). Sort a copy, assign ranks using a hash map, then substitute back. Handles duplicates by giving them the same rank.

questions.name: Task Scheduler
questions.description: Given a list of tasks with cooldown n, find the minimum intervals the CPU needs to finish all tasks. Count task frequencies — the answer is max(total tasks, (max_freq - 1) * (n + 1) + count_of_max_freq_tasks).

questions.name: Implement Stack using Arrays
constraints: At most 100 calls to push, pop, top, and empty
questions.description: Implement a last-in-first-out stack with push, pop, top, and empty operations using only two queues. The push operation is O(n) — enqueue to queue2, move all of queue1 to queue2, then swap queue1 and queue2.

questions.name: Implement Queue using Arrays
questions.description: Implement a first-in-first-out queue with push, pop, peek, and empty operations using only two stacks. The amortized O(1) approach: push to stack1; on pop/peek, lazily transfer stack1 to stack2 only when stack2 is empty.

questions.name: Implement Stack using Queue
questions.description: Implement a stack using only queues. Push is O(n): enqueue the new element, then rotate all previously enqueued elements to the back so the new element sits at the front for O(1) pop.

questions.name: Valid Parentheses
questions.description: Given a string s containing only '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. A string is valid if every open bracket is closed by the same type in correct order. Use a stack — push opens, match on closes.

questions.name: Next Greater Element
questions.description: Given two arrays nums1 and nums2 where nums1 is a subset of nums2, for each element in nums1 find its next greater element in nums2. Use a monotonic decreasing stack on nums2 and a hash map to store results.

questions.name: Sort a Stack
questions.description: Sort a stack in ascending order using recursion, with the smallest element on top. Recursively remove all elements, sort the smaller stack, and insert each element back in its correct sorted position.

questions.name: Next Smaller Element
questions.description: For each element in an array, find the nearest smaller element to its left. Use a monotonic increasing stack — for each new element, pop elements that are ≥ it; the current stack top is the answer.

questions.name: LRU Cache
questions.description: Design a data structure that follows the Least Recently Used cache eviction policy with O(1) get and put operations. Implemented using a doubly linked list (for O(1) removal/insertion) combined with a hash map (for O(1) lookup).

questions.name: LFU Cache
questions.description: Design a data structure for a Least Frequently Used cache. Uses two hash maps and a doubly linked list per frequency level — one map for key→node, one for frequency→DLL. On tie in frequency, evict the least recently used.

questions.name: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
questions.description: Given an array of integers heights representing the histogram's bar heights, find the area of the largest rectangle in the histogram. Use a monotonic increasing stack — for each bar, calculate the max rectangle it can be the shortest bar of.

questions.name: Sliding Window Maximum
questions.description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the maximum value in each sliding window of size k. Uses a monotonic decreasing deque — maintain indices of potentially useful elements, always removing elements outside the window.

questions.name: Implement Min Stack
constraints: Methods pop, top, getMin called on non-empty stack
questions.description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in O(1) time. Use an auxiliary stack that tracks the current minimum — push to min-stack whenever the new element is ≤ current minimum.

questions.name: Rotten Oranges (using BFS)
questions.description: Given an m×n grid where 0=empty, 1=fresh orange, 2=rotten orange, return the minimum time for all fresh oranges to rot. BFS from all initially rotten oranges simultaneously. If any fresh orange remains unreachable, return -1.

questions.name: Stock Span Problem
questions.description: Design a class to collect daily stock prices and return the span of the stock's price for the current day. The span is the number of consecutive days (including today) where the stock price was ≤ today's price. Uses a monotonic stack.

questions.name: The Celebrity Problem
questions.description: Among n people at a party, a celebrity is known by everyone but knows nobody. Given a function knows(a,b), find the celebrity (or -1). Optimal: eliminate non-celebrities with a single pass, then verify the candidate.

questions.name: Sum of Subarray Minimum
questions.description: Given an array of integers, find the sum of min(subarray) over all subarrays. For each element, find how many subarrays it is the minimum of using previous smaller and next smaller element via monotonic stack.

questions.name: Asteroid Collision
questions.description: Given an array of integers representing asteroids in a row (positive = right, negative = left, same absolute value = same size), find the state after all collisions. Use a stack — a collision occurs when a negative asteroid meets a positive one on the stack top.

questions.name: Reverse Words in a String
constraints: s contains English letters, digits, and spaces
questions.description: Given an input string s, reverse the order of the words. A word is a sequence of non-space characters. Words are separated by at least one space. The result should not have leading/trailing spaces or multiple spaces between words.

questions.name: Longest Palindrome in a String
constraints: s consists of only digits and English letters
questions.description: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring. The expand-around-center approach runs in O(n²) — for each character (and each pair), expand outward while characters match. Manacher's algorithm achieves O(n).

questions.name: Roman to Integer
questions.description: Convert a Roman numeral string to an integer. Roman numerals use subtraction when a smaller value precedes a larger one (e.g., IV=4). Parse right-to-left or use a map — subtract if current value < previous value, else add.

questions.name: String to Integer (atoi)
constraints: s consists of English letters, digits, ' ', '+', '-', and '.'
questions.description: Implement the myAtoi function that converts a string to a 32-bit signed integer. Handle leading whitespace, optional sign, digit characters, non-digit stop, and clamp the result to [-2^31, 2^31-1].

questions.name: Count and Say
questions.description: The count-and-say sequence is a sequence of digit strings defined by a recursive formula. countAndSay(1) = '1', and each subsequent term describes the previous term (e.g., '1' → one 1 → '11' → two 1s → '21' → ...).

questions.name: Longest Common Prefix
constraints: strs[i] consists of only lower-case English letters
questions.description: Given an array of strings strs, find the longest common prefix among all strings. Compare character by character — the LCP can only be as long as the shortest string. Can also sort and compare only first and last.

questions.name: Rabin-Karp Algorithm
constraints: a and b consist of lowercase English letters
questions.description: Given two strings a and b, find the minimum number of times a must be repeated so that b is a substring of the result. Apply the Rabin-Karp rolling hash algorithm for efficient substring matching, or KMP for O(n+m) matching.

questions.name: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
constraints: s consists of only uppercase English letters
questions.description: Given a string s and an integer k, find the length of the longest substring containing the same letter after replacing at most k characters. Sliding window — maintain a window where (length - max_freq) ≤ k.

questions.name: Minimum Window Substring
constraints: s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters
questions.description: Given strings s and t, find the minimum window substring of s that contains all characters of t. Classic sliding window with two pointers and a frequency map — expand right to include all of t, then shrink left.

questions.name: Valid Anagram
constraints: s and t consist of lowercase English letters
questions.description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s. Two strings are anagrams if they contain the same characters with the same frequencies. Sort both strings, or use a frequency count array of size 26.

questions.name: Count of Anagrams
constraints: s and p consist of lowercase English letters
questions.description: Given strings s and p, find all start indices of p's anagrams in s. Sliding window of size len(p) — use a frequency array difference counter to check if the current window is an anagram of p in O(1) per step.

questions.name: Inorder Traversal
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100]
questions.description: Traverse a binary tree in inorder (left-root-right). The iterative approach uses a stack: push nodes going left until null, then process and move right. Inorder of a BST yields a sorted sequence.

questions.name: Preorder Traversal
questions.description: Traverse a binary tree in preorder (root-left-right). Iterative approach: push root to stack, pop and process, then push right child first, then left child (so left is processed first).

questions.name: Postorder Traversal
questions.description: Traverse a binary tree in postorder (left-right-root). Iterative approach 1: reverse of a modified preorder (root-right-left). Approach 2: two stacks. These orderings are fundamental for tree evaluation and serialization.

questions.name: Morris Inorder Traversal
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100]
questions.description: Perform inorder traversal of a binary tree without using recursion or a stack, using O(1) extra space. Morris traversal creates temporary right links (threaded tree) to return to ancestors after visiting left subtrees.

questions.name: Morris Preorder Traversal
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100]
questions.description: Perform preorder traversal (root-left-right) without recursion or a stack using O(1) space. Morris preorder uses the same threading technique as Morris inorder but visits (prints) the node before threading the left subtree.

questions.name: Left/Right View of Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100]
questions.description: Return the values visible from the right side of a binary tree (rightmost node at each depth level). BFS level-order traversal — record the last node's value at each level. Also commonly asked for left side view.

questions.name: Top View of Binary Tree
questions.description: Return the nodes visible when looking at a binary tree from directly above. BFS with horizontal distance tracking — for each node, map its horizontal distance to its value (only keep the first node seen per distance).

questions.name: Bottom View of Binary Tree
questions.description: Return the nodes visible when looking at a binary tree from directly below. BFS with horizontal distance — similar to top view, but keep updating the map so the last (deepest) node per horizontal distance wins.

questions.name: Zigzag Traversal (Level Order)
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000]
questions.description: Return the level-order traversal of a binary tree's values but alternating direction: left-to-right for even levels, right-to-left for odd levels. BFS with a boolean flag, using deque (push_front vs push_back).

questions.name: Boundary Traversal of Binary Tree
questions.description: Return all nodes on the boundary of a binary tree in anti-clockwise order: root, left boundary (excluding leaves), all leaves (left to right), right boundary (excluding leaves, bottom to top).

questions.name: Vertical Order Traversal
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 1000]
questions.description: Return the vertical order traversal of a binary tree. Nodes are sorted by column, then row, then value. BFS with (column, row) tracking — group nodes by column using a map, sort within each column.

questions.name: Root to Node Path in Binary Tree
questions.description: Find all root-to-leaf paths in a binary tree. DFS backtracking — maintain a current path list, add each node as you recurse down, record the path on reaching a leaf, and remove the node when backtracking.

questions.name: Maximum Path Sum
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 3 * 10^4]
questions.description: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree where the path can start and end at any node. For each node, compute the best single-branch contribution (max of left, right, or 0), and update global max with left + root + right.

questions.name: Construct Binary Tree from Inorder and Preorder
questions.description: Construct a binary tree from preorder and inorder traversal arrays. The preorder root is the first element; find it in inorder to split left and right subtrees. Use a hash map for O(1) inorder index lookup.

questions.name: Construct Binary Tree from Inorder and Postorder
questions.description: Construct a binary tree from inorder and postorder traversal arrays. The postorder root is the last element; find it in inorder to split subtrees. Build right subtree before left (postorder processes right before left).

questions.name: Symmetric Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 1000]
questions.description: Check if a binary tree is symmetric (mirror image of itself). Recursively check if left subtree is a mirror of right subtree: compare root values and recursively check (left.left, right.right) and (left.right, right.left).

questions.name: Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000]
questions.description: Flatten a binary tree to a linked list using the tree's right pointers in-place (preorder order). The elegant reverse-postorder approach: process right, then left, keep track of previously processed node and set it as current right.

questions.name: Check for Children Sum Property
questions.description: In a tree satisfying the children sum property, every node's value equals the sum of its two children's values. Verify this recursively. A variant: modify the tree to satisfy this property by only increasing node values.

questions.name: Nodes at Distance K in Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 500]
questions.description: Find all nodes in a binary tree at distance k from a target node. Build a parent pointer map (BFS/DFS), then BFS from the target node treating all edges (parent/child) as undirected, stopping at distance k.

questions.name: Minimum time to Burn the Binary Tree from a Node

test_cases.input: tree = 1 2 3 4 5 null 7 8 null 6 null null 9, target = 8

questions.description: Given a binary tree and a target node, find the minimum time to burn the entire tree if fire starts at the target. Equivalent to finding the maximum distance from the target to any other node — convert to graph and BFS.

questions.name: Count Complete Tree Nodes
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 5 * 10^4]
questions.description: Count nodes in a complete binary tree in O(log²n) time. For a perfect binary tree, count = 2^h - 1. Recursively check if left and right subtrees are perfect by comparing leftmost and rightmost heights.

questions.name: Requirements needed to construct a Unique Binary Tree
questions.description: Determine which pairs of traversals (preorder, inorder, postorder, level-order) are sufficient to uniquely reconstruct a binary tree. Any traversal combined with inorder is sufficient; postorder + preorder alone is not.

questions.name: LCA in Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [2, 10^5]
questions.description: Find the lowest common ancestor of two nodes p and q in a binary tree. If root equals p or q, return root. Otherwise recurse on both subtrees — if both return non-null, root is the LCA; otherwise return the non-null result.

questions.name: Kth Ancestor in a Tree
questions.description: Given a rooted tree, for each query find the kth ancestor of a given node. Preprocess using binary lifting: ancestor[i][j] = 2^j-th ancestor of node i. Answer each query in O(log n) by combining jumps.

questions.name: Maximum Width of Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 3000]
questions.description: Find the maximum width of a binary tree (maximum number of nodes in any level). Level-order BFS — assign indices to nodes (left child = 2*i, right child = 2*i+1), width = last_index - first_index + 1 per level.

questions.name: Height of Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4]
questions.description: Find the maximum depth (number of nodes along the longest path from root to a leaf). Recursively: maxDepth = 1 + max(maxDepth(left), maxDepth(right)). Base case: null node has depth 0.

questions.name: Check for Balanced Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 5000]
questions.description: Check if a binary tree is height-balanced (every node's left and right subtrees differ in height by at most 1). Combine height computation and balance check in a single DFS — return -1 as sentinel for unbalanced subtrees.

questions.name: Populate Next Right Pointers
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2^12 - 1]
questions.description: Given a perfect binary tree where all leaves are at the same level, connect each node's next pointer to its right neighbor. Iterative level-by-level: for each node at level l, link node.left.next = node.right and node.right.next = node.next.left.

questions.name: Search in a BST
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 5000]
questions.description: Given the root of a BST and an integer val, find the node with value equal to val. Since BST is ordered, if val < root.val go left, if val > root.val go right. O(log n) on average for balanced BSTs.

questions.name: Construct BST from given keys
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree will be in the range [0, 10^4]
questions.description: Given the root of a BST and a value to insert, return the root of the BST after insertion. Find the correct position using BST ordering: go left if value < current, right if value > current, insert as a leaf.

questions.name: Construct a BST from a Preorder Traversal
constraints: All values in preorder are distinct
questions.description: Given an array of integers representing the preorder traversal of a BST, construct the BST. Iterative approach uses a stack: maintain a stack of nodes; if next value < stack top, it's the left child; else pop until parent found.

questions.name: Inorder Successor/Predecessor in BST
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 10^4]
questions.description: Find the inorder successor (next greater node in BST order) of a given node p. Without parent pointers: traverse BST keeping track of the last node seen with value > p.val — that's the successor.

questions.name: Floor in a BST
questions.description: Find the largest value in the BST that is less than or equal to the given key (floor). Standard BST traversal: if key equals current, it's the floor. If key < current, floor is in left subtree. If key > current, record current and search right.

questions.name: Ceil in a BST
questions.description: Find the smallest value in the BST that is greater than or equal to the given key (ceiling). Mirror of floor: if key equals current, it's the ceil. If key > current, ceil is in right subtree. If key < current, record current and search left.

questions.name: Find Kth Smallest / Largest Element in BST
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is n
questions.description: Find the kth smallest element in a BST. The elegant solution: inorder traversal of BST gives sorted order — count nodes during inorder and return the kth one. Also solvable with augmented BST for O(log n) per query.

questions.name: Find a pair with a given sum in BST
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 10^4]
questions.description: Given a BST and a target k, find if there exist two elements in the BST whose sum is equal to k. Use inorder traversal to get sorted array + two pointers, or use a hash set during traversal, or use BST iterator from both ends.

questions.name: BST Iterator
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 10^5]
questions.description: Implement an iterator over a BST that yields elements in ascending (inorder) order. The O(h) space solution uses a stack — push all left nodes initially; on next(), pop top, push all left nodes of its right child.

questions.name: Size of Largest BST in Binary Tree
questions.description: Find the size of the largest BST subtree within a given binary tree. For each subtree, return (min, max, size, isBST) — a subtree is a BST if both children are BSTs and values respect BST ordering.

questions.name: Delete Node in a BST
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4]
questions.description: Delete a node with a given key from a BST. Three cases: (1) node has no children — simply remove; (2) node has one child — replace with child; (3) node has two children — replace value with inorder successor, delete successor.

questions.name: Recover BST | Correct BST with two nodes swapped
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [2, 1000]
questions.description: Two nodes of a BST are swapped by mistake. Recover the BST without changing its structure. Inorder traversal finds the two misplaced nodes: first violation is (prev, curr) when prev > curr; second violation updates the second node.

questions.name: Merge Two BSTs
questions.description: Merge two BSTs into a sorted list (or a balanced BST). Optimal: inorder traverse both BSTs simultaneously using two stacks (like merging two sorted arrays), merge in O(m+n) time and O(h1+h2) space.

questions.name: Two Sum in BST
questions.description: Given a BST and a target, determine if two distinct nodes sum to the target. Uses BST iterator from both ends (one forward, one backward) for O(n) time O(h) space, similar to two-pointer on a sorted array.

questions.name: Diameter of Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 10^4]
questions.description: Find the diameter of a binary tree — the length of the longest path between any two nodes (the path may not pass through the root). For each node, the diameter passing through it = left_height + right_height. Track the global maximum.

questions.name: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 3 * 10^4]
questions.description: Find the maximum sum path in a binary tree where a path can pass through any node. Key insight: for each node, compute max gain from left and right subtrees (clamped at 0), update global max, and return the single-branch max to parent.

questions.name: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4]
questions.description: Design an algorithm to serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize that string back to the tree. Level-order (BFS) serialization with null markers is straightforward; preorder recursion with '#' for nulls is elegant.

questions.name: Morris Traversal
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100]
questions.description: Morris traversal allows inorder (or preorder) tree traversal using O(1) space by temporarily modifying the tree structure. It uses the concept of threaded binary trees — right null pointers are used as temporary links back to inorder successors.

questions.name: Flatten BST to Sorted List
constraints: The number of nodes in the given tree will be in the range [1, 100]
questions.description: Given a BST, rearrange it into a right-skewed tree (linked list) where nodes are in ascending order. Inorder traversal builds the sorted list; alternatively, the elegant approach rewires pointers during traversal without extra space.

questions.name: Count BST Nodes in Range
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 2 * 10^4]
questions.description: Given a BST and a range [low, high], return the sum of all node values within that range. Prune the search: if node.val < low, only search right subtree; if node.val > high, only search left subtree; else add current and search both.

questions.name: Clone a Graph
constraints: The number of nodes in the graph is in the range [0, 100]
questions.description: Given a reference to a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy of the graph. Use DFS or BFS with a hash map from original node to its clone — check the map before creating a new clone to handle cycles.

questions.name: DFS
questions.description: Depth-First Search traversal of a graph. From the source node, visit as far as possible along each branch before backtracking. Uses recursion or an explicit stack. Returns nodes in DFS order.

questions.name: BFS
questions.description: Breadth-First Search traversal of a graph. Visit all neighbors of the current level before moving to the next. Uses a queue. Returns nodes level by level — useful for finding shortest paths in unweighted graphs.

questions.name: Detect Cycle in Undirected Graph
questions.description: Detect if an undirected graph has a cycle using BFS or DFS. DFS: if we visit an already-visited node that is not the parent, a cycle exists. BFS: similar with tracking parents. Also solvable with Union-Find.

questions.name: Detect Cycle in Directed Graph
questions.description: Detect if a directed graph has a cycle using DFS with a recursion stack. A cycle exists if we visit a node that is already in the current DFS path (recursion stack). Colors: WHITE=unvisited, GRAY=in stack, BLACK=done.

questions.name: Topological Sorting (BFS/Kahn's)
questions.description: Topological sort of a DAG using Kahn's BFS algorithm. Compute in-degrees, enqueue all 0-in-degree nodes, process queue: add to result, decrement neighbors' in-degrees, enqueue newly 0-in-degree nodes. If result size < V, cycle exists.

questions.name: Topological Sorting (DFS)
questions.description: Topological sort using DFS. Post-order DFS: after fully exploring a node's subtree, push it to a stack. Pop the stack for the topological order. Only valid on DAGs — run cycle detection first.

questions.name: Number of Islands
questions.description: Count the number of islands in a 2D grid of '0's and '1's. DFS or BFS from each unvisited '1' — mark the entire connected component as visited ('0'). Count the number of times you trigger a new DFS/BFS.

questions.name: Bipartite Check (BFS/DFS)
questions.description: Check if a graph is bipartite — can it be 2-colored so no two adjacent nodes share the same color? BFS/DFS coloring: assign alternating colors, fail if any edge connects same-colored nodes. Equivalently, no odd-length cycle exists.

questions.name: Strongly Connected Components (Kosaraju's)
questions.description: Find all Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) in a directed graph using Kosaraju's two-pass DFS algorithm: first DFS on original graph to get finish order; second DFS on transposed graph in reverse finish order.

questions.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
questions.description: Find shortest paths from a source to all vertices in a weighted graph with non-negative edges. Uses a min-heap (priority queue) — greedily process the unvisited vertex with smallest tentative distance and relax its neighbors.

questions.name: Bellman-Ford Algorithm
questions.description: Find shortest paths from a source vertex in a graph that may have negative edge weights (but no negative cycles). Relax all edges V-1 times. On the Vth relaxation, if any edge still relaxes, a negative cycle exists.

questions.name: Floyd Warshall
questions.description: Find shortest paths between all pairs of vertices in a weighted graph. Dynamic programming: for each intermediate vertex k, update dist[i][j] = min(dist[i][j], dist[i][k] + dist[k][j]). Handles negative weights but not negative cycles.

questions.name: Minimum Spanning Tree (Prim's)
questions.description: Find the Minimum Spanning Tree of a weighted undirected graph using Prim's algorithm. Greedy: start from any vertex, always add the cheapest edge connecting the MST to a non-MST vertex. Use a min-heap for efficiency.

questions.name: Minimum Spanning Tree (Kruskal's)
questions.description: Find the Minimum Spanning Tree using Kruskal's algorithm. Sort all edges by weight, then greedily add edges that don't form a cycle (checked using Union-Find / Disjoint Set Union). O(E log E) time.

questions.name: Number of Ways to Arrive at Destination
questions.description: Count the number of ways to travel from node 0 to node n-1 in a weighted graph using the shortest path. Dijkstra's modified: also track the number of shortest paths to each node — update count when a shorter or equal path is found.

questions.name: Disjoint Set (Union-Find)
questions.description: Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union) data structure with path compression and union by rank. Supports two operations: find(x) returns the representative of x's set; union(x, y) merges the sets containing x and y.

questions.name: Course Schedule (Cycle Detection / Topo Sort)
questions.description: Given n courses and prerequisites, determine if it's possible to finish all courses. Build a directed graph, then check for cycles using topological sort (Kahn's BFS) or DFS — a cycle means it's impossible.

questions.name: Maximum Product Subarray
questions.description: Find the maximum product of a contiguous subarray. Unlike Kadane's, you must track both the maximum and minimum product ending at each position (because a negative × negative = positive can be the maximum).

questions.name: Longest Increasing Subsequence
questions.description: Find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence. O(n log n) approach uses a patience sorting technique with binary search — maintain a list 'tails' where tails[i] is the smallest tail of all increasing subsequences of length i+1.

questions.name: Longest Common Subsequence
constraints: text1 and text2 consist of only lowercase English characters
questions.description: Find the length of the longest common subsequence of two strings. Classic 2D DP — dp[i][j] = LCS of text1[0..i-1] and text2[0..j-1]. If characters match, dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i-1][j-1]; else max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

questions.name: 0/1 Knapsack
questions.description: Given items with weights and values and a capacity W, maximize the total value without exceeding capacity, where each item can be taken at most once. 2D DP: dp[i][w] = max value using first i items with capacity w.

questions.name: Edit Distance
constraints: word1 and word2 consist of lowercase English letters
questions.description: Given two words, find the minimum number of single-character operations (insert, delete, replace) to convert one into the other. 2D DP: dp[i][j] = edit distance between first i chars of word1 and first j chars of word2.

questions.name: Maximum Sum Increasing Subsequence
questions.description: Find the maximum sum of an increasing subsequence in an array. Similar to LIS but track sum instead of length — dp[i] = max sum of increasing subsequence ending at index i. Answer is max(dp[i]).

questions.name: Matrix Chain Multiplication
questions.description: Find the minimum number of scalar multiplications to compute the product of a chain of matrices. Interval DP — dp[i][j] = min cost to multiply matrices from i to j. Split at each k: dp[i][j] = min(dp[i][k] + dp[k+1][j] + cost).

questions.name: Minimum Insertions/Deletions to Convert String A to String B
constraints: word1 and word2 consist of lowercase English letters
questions.description: Given two strings, find the minimum number of insertions and deletions to convert string A to string B. Answer = len(A) + len(B) - 2 * LCS(A, B), where LCS is the longest common subsequence.

questions.name: Longest String Chain
constraints: words[i] consists of only lowercase English letters
questions.description: Find the length of the longest string chain. A chain is valid if each word can be formed by deleting exactly one character from the next. Sort by length, then LIS-style DP — dp[word] = max chain length ending at word.

questions.name: Longest Bitonic Subsequence
questions.description: Find the length of the longest bitonic subsequence — a subsequence that first increases then decreases. Compute LIS ending at each index (left to right) and LDS starting at each index (right to left); answer = max(LIS[i] + LDS[i] - 1).

questions.name: Count Distinct Subsequences
constraints: s and t consist of English letters
questions.description: Count the number of distinct subsequences of string s that equal string t. 2D DP: dp[i][j] = number of ways to form t[0..j-1] from s[0..i-1]. If s[i-1] == t[j-1]: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + dp[i-1][j]; else dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j].

questions.name: Egg Drop Problem
questions.description: Given k eggs and n floors, find the minimum number of trials needed in the worst case to determine the critical floor. DP with binary search or mathematical approach using dp[t][k] = number of floors testable in t trials with k eggs.

questions.name: Burst Balloons
questions.description: Given an array of balloons with values, burst all balloons to maximize coins. Bursting balloon i gives nums[i-1]*nums[i]*nums[i+1] coins. Interval DP — think of it as choosing the LAST balloon to burst in each range.

questions.name: Evaluate Boolean Expression to True
questions.description: Given a boolean expression with T, F, &, |, ^ operators, count the number of ways to parenthesize it to evaluate to True. Interval DP — for each operator, count how many ways left part is T/F and right part is T/F and combine.

questions.name: Palindrome Partitioning (min cuts)
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters only
questions.description: Given a string s, partition it into palindromes using the minimum number of cuts. DP: first precompute isPalin[i][j] for all substrings, then dp[i] = min cuts for s[0..i]. dp[i] = min(dp[j-1] + 1) for all j ≤ i where s[j..i] is palindrome.

questions.name: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
constraints: All input consists of lowercase English letters
questions.description: Implement a Trie (prefix tree) that supports insert, search (exact match), and startsWith (prefix check) operations. Each node stores an array of 26 child pointers and a boolean isEnd. All operations are O(L) where L is the word length.

questions.name: Implement Trie II (Count occurrences)
questions.description: Extended Trie that also tracks how many times a word has been inserted and supports deletion (erase). Each node stores countEndHere (words ending here) and countPrefix (words passing through). Decrement on erase.

questions.name: Longest String with All Prefixes
questions.description: Find the longest word in a dictionary such that every prefix of the word is also in the dictionary. Insert all words into a Trie, then DFS/BFS — a word is valid only if every prefix node is marked as a complete word.

questions.name: Number of Distinct Substrings in a String
questions.description: Count the number of distinct substrings of a string using a Trie. Insert every suffix of the string into the Trie, counting new edges added — each new edge represents a new distinct substring. O(n²) time and space.

questions.name: Power Set (Important)
questions.description: Generate all possible subsets (the power set) of an array with unique elements. Bit manipulation: for a set of n elements, iterate from 0 to 2^n-1 and include element i if bit i is set in the mask. Or use backtracking.

questions.name: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
questions.description: Find the maximum XOR of any two numbers in an array. Insert all numbers into a binary Trie (bit by bit from MSB to LSB). For each number, greedily traverse the Trie choosing the opposite bit at each level to maximize XOR.

questions.name: Maximum XOR With an Element From Array
questions.description: Given an array nums and queries [xi, mi], for each query find the maximum XOR of xi with any element in nums that is ≤ mi. Offline approach: sort both queries and nums, process queries in order of mi, inserting into Trie as elements become available.
